title: BiocAnnotationLab: developing new approaches to genomic annotation curation
text: Bioconductor’s annotation resources extensive can challenging maintain various reasons. package collects information relevant reducing complexity annotation management taking advantage new approaches data representation supporting “self-service” solutions needing rapid revision annotation resources Basic facts: Package downloaded 20000 distinct IPs per month powered 70MB SQLite database 14 tables following bespoke schema production GO.db package mixed production numerous annotation packages BioconductorAnnotationPipeline system. parquet-based representation equivalent content can fit 7MB disk. See GO.db3 note OBO-parquet transformation resource BiocGOprep. Yet another approach Gene Ontology (indeed ontologies OBOFoundry) leverages semantic SQL – see ontoProc2, development. org.*.eg.db based curation resources NCBI need carved different organisms; see RNCBIgene package development. Project-specific resources like SGD, TAIR, PLASMO special casing pipeline, needs fresh, maintainable approach architect still project Tooling move SQLite parquet available produces 7x reduction footprint Upstream tasks well-documented, reviewed regularly

title: BiocAnnotationLab: developing new approaches to genomic annotation curation
text: Based download counts, GO.db popular resource Bioconductor. ’s basic use case, taken sources goana.R limma. Try : GO.db self-descriptive. can learn underlying database. GO.db presents environments traversals ontological hierarchy.
code: TERM <- suppressMessages(AnnotationDbi::select(GO.db::GO.db,keys=GOID,columns="TERM")) GOID = c("GO:0001960", "GO:0001961", "GO:0010803", "GO:0060334", "GO:0060338",  "GO:0070099", "GO:0070103", "GO:0070107", "GO:0070758", "GO:1900234",  "GO:1902205", "GO:1902211", "GO:1902214", "GO:1902226", "GO:1903881",  "GO:2000446", "GO:2000492", "GO:2000659") library(GO.db) TERM <- suppressMessages(AnnotationDbi::select(GO.db::GO.db,keys=GOID,columns="TERM")) head(TERM) ##         GOID                                                           TERM ## 1 GO:0001960     negative regulation of cytokine-mediated signaling pathway ## 2 GO:0001961     positive regulation of cytokine-mediated signaling pathway ## 3 GO:0010803 regulation of tumor necrosis factor-mediated signaling pathway ## 4 GO:0060334    regulation of type II interferon-mediated signaling pathway ## 5 GO:0060338     regulation of type I interferon-mediated signaling pathway ## title: BiocAnnotationLab: developing new approaches to genomic annotation curation
text: pursuit proof concept, packages GO.db2 (SQLite-based) GO.db3 produced. GO.db2 uses simplified schema tables; GO.db3 uses parquet representations GO.db’s tables. avoid conflict dplyr’s select, GO.db3 defines select3 emulate AnnotationDbi::select. can learn underlying resources, extdata/go323 observe sharp reduction size data footprint. wrap hierarchy-oriented environments functions now. environments computed “fly”; produced.
code: library(GO.db3) chk = select3("GO.db3",keys=GOID, columns=c("GOID", "TERM"), keytype="GOID") head(chk) ##         GOID                                                           TERM ## 1 GO:0001960     negative regulation of cytokine-mediated signaling pathway ## 2 GO:0001961     positive regulation of cytokine-mediated signaling pathway ## 3 GO:0010803 regulation of tumor necrosis factor-mediated signaling pathway ## 4 GO:0060334    regulation of type II interferon-mediated signaling pathway ## 5 GO:0060338     regulation of type I interferon-mediated signaling pathway ## 6 GO:0070099             regulation of chemokine-mediated signaling pathway pfns = dir(system.file("extdata", "go323", package="GO.db3"), full=TRUE) head(basename(pfns)) ## [1] "go_bp_offspring.parquet" "go_bp_parents.parquet"   ## [3] "go_cc_offspring.parquet" "go_cc_parents.parquet"   ## [5] "go_mf_offspring.parquet" "go_mf_parents.parquet" sum(file.size(pfns)) ## [1] 7329583 get("GO:0001960", GO.db3::GOBPPARENTS()) ##                 is_a                 is_a                 is_a  ##         "GO:0001959"         "GO:0009968"         "GO:0060761"  ## negatively_regulates  ##         "GO:0019221"

what: ontoProc2 for GO: using Semantic SQL
title: BiocAnnotationLab: developing new approaches to genomic annotation curation
text: INCAtools semantic SQL project converts ontologies OWL OBO formats SQLite. README remarks ’s can use Semantic SQL representation GO ontoProc2. GOID vector defined . Note full representation facts relationships GO entails large footprint.
code: SQLite provides many advantages  - files can be downloaded and subsequently queried without network latency  - compared to querying a static rdf, owl, or obo file, there is no startup/parse delay  - robust and performant  - excellent support in many languages Although the focus is on SQLite, this library can also be used for other  DBMSs like PostgreSQL, MySQL, Oracle, etc library(ontoProc2) gosem = retrieve_semsql_conn("go") chk1 = dplyr::tbl(gosem, "statements") |>    dplyr::filter(subject %in% GOID, predicate=="rdfs:label") |>    dplyr::select(subject, value) head(chk1) ## # Source:   SQL [?? x 2] ## # Database: sqlite 3.51.2 [/Users/vincentcarey/Library/Caches/org.R-project.R/R/BiocFileCache/40e293b372b_go.db] ##   subject    value                                                          ##   <chr>      <chr>                                                          ## 1 GO:0001960 negative regulation of cytokine-mediated signaling pathway     ## 2 GO:0001961 positive regulation of cytokine-mediated signaling pathway     ## 3 GO:0010803 regulation of tumor necrosis factor-mediated signaling pathway ## 4 GO:0060334 regulation of type II interferon-mediated signaling pathway    ## 5 GO:0060338 regulation of type I interferon-mediated signaling pathway     ## 6 GO:0070099 regulation of chemokine-mediated signaling pathway file.size(slot(gosem, "dbname")) ## [1] 1694437376

title: BiocAnnotationLab: developing new approaches to genomic annotation curation
text: AnnotationDbi’s production GO.db involves complex pipeline processes intermingled address multiple objectives. AnnotationDbi’s “select” method conceptual conflict widely used select method dplyr. GO.db3 near-complete functionally compatible replacement GO.db. Production GO.db3 independent annotation production processes; parquet production OBO documented BiocGOprep. GO.db3::select3 provided emulate AnnotationDbi::select, interrogation interfaces based parquet representation devised. BiocGOprep produces parquet files following GO.db’s schema; essential adopted simplify producing compatibility legacy approaches. GO.db GO.db3 present limited view relationships encoded Gene Ontology; complete view made available SQL-based interrogation via ontoProc2. ontoProc2 helps provide full programmatic access ontology managed Semantic SQL project. useful work exercises based rols, help understand added value specific curation packaging.
